exceedCredits.question: What happens if I exceed my credits?
exceedCredits.answer: You can upgrade your subscription, add a credit pack to your existing plan via the slider, or buy a one-time Pay As You Go top-up ($10 / $50 / $100). PAYG credits persist across renewal cycles and are consumed after your subscription credits run out.

changePlans.question: Can I change plans at any time?
changePlans.answer: Yes, you can upgrade at any time. To downgrade, you'll need to wait until the end of your current billing period.

payAsYouGo.question: How does Pay As You Go work?
payAsYouGo.answer: Pay As You Go is a one-time top-up - pick a $10 / $50 / $100 tier and your wallet is credited with 8,000 / 40,000 / 80,000 credits (≈ $1.25 per 1,000 credits). No subscription required, credits persist across renewal cycles and are consumed only after your subscription credits run out.

sharedStorage.question: Is storage shared between users?
sharedStorage.answer: No, each account has its own storage space. For teams, storage can be shared among members depending on your plan and needs.

interfaceMappings.actionNameInfo: A CSS selector targeting the HTML element (e.g. #search-form, .submit-btn), or a data-action attribute name as fallback.
interfaceMappings.actionMappingInfoDesc: Map interface actions to targets. Each row links an action name (data-action attribute) to a target node or control.
interfaceMappings.actionMappingInfoAction: Action name: use data-action="name" on buttons/forms in your HTML template.
interfaceMappings.actionMappingInfoTarget: Nodes: fires a trigger or navigates to an interface. Pagination: prev/next items. Send Signal: resolves the awaiting interface signal.
interfaceMappings.actionMappingInfoTypes: Action type is inferred automatically from the target node.
interfaceMappings.variableMappingInfoDesc: Map each template variable to a workflow data source. Variables declared in your HTML template appear here automatically after saving.
interfaceMappings.variableMappingInfoTemplate: Each variable corresponds to a placeholder in your template
interfaceMappings.variableMappingInfoDrag: Drag a field from a parent node output to auto-fill the expression
interfaceMappings.variableMappingInfoExpression: Or type the expression manually (e.g. mcp:step.output.data.title)
interfaceMappings.jsDataDesc: In run mode, all resolved variables are available via window.__RESOLVED_DATA__. Use this instead of JSON.parse from DOM elements.
interfaceMappings.fileDisplayDesc: After a download_file node, use the canonical `file` output in your HTML template. The frontend auto-injects an auth token (logged-in app) or an HMAC signature (marketplace + share preview).
interfaceMappings.outputFieldsDescription: Form fields detected in HTML. Available to downstream nodes after form submit.
interfaceMappings.noFormFields: No form fields detected. Add <input>, <select>, or <textarea> with name attributes.
interfaceMappings.entryInterfaceDescription: Show this interface first when multiple interfaces are pending.
interfaceMappings.generateScreenshotDescription: When enabled, captures a PNG of the rendered interface and exposes it as the screenshot FileRef output for downstream nodes (email attachments, Telegram photo, agent image input). Best-effort: capture failures leave the output absent without failing the workflow.
interfaceMappings.generatePdfDescription: When enabled, renders the interface to a PDF and exposes it as the pdf FileRef output for downstream nodes (email attachment, Telegram document, agent file input). Best-effort: render failures leave the output absent without failing the workflow.
interfaceMappings.rendererUnavailable: Screenshot and PDF rendering require the optional renderer component, which is not running on this installation. These toggles will produce no output until an administrator enables it:
interfaceMappings.exposeRenderedSourceDescription: When enabled, exposes the resolved interface templates as the rendered_html, rendered_css, rendered_js string outputs for downstream nodes (email body, agent text input, debugging). Best-effort: render failures leave the outputs absent without failing the workflow.

errorTrigger.title: Error Trigger
errorTrigger.description: Fires when the watched workflow run ends in FAILED or PARTIAL_SUCCESS. That covers any kind of failure: an unhandled node exception (HTTP error, code crash, tool throw), a stop_on_error node, a timeout, or partial-success when some branches failed but others succeeded. Use it to alert ops, log failures, retry, or fall back. Anti-loop: if this handler itself fails, no further error handler is triggered.
errorTrigger.watchedWorkflowHint: This handler fires whenever the workflow above fails - any node exception, stop_on_error, timeout, or partial-success ends in FAILED/PARTIAL_SUCCESS and dispatches here.
errorTrigger.bootstrapDescription: Click Run on this handler ONCE after saving so a WAITING_TRIGGER run exists. Without that initial run, the dispatcher silently drops the first failure (logged as ‘No active run, skipping dispatch’).

chatConfig.executionTimeoutInfo: Maximum time (seconds) the agent can run before being stopped.
chatConfig.inactivityTimeoutInfo: Force-stop the agent if it produces no output (no token, tool call, or result) for this many seconds. 0 disables it; 10-7200s. Separate from Timeout, which is the total run budget.
chatConfig.toolsModeInfo: Controls which tools the agent has access to.
chatConfig.webSearchInfo: Allows the agent to search the web when it needs up-to-date information.
chatConfig.imageGenerationInfo: Lets the agent generate images via OpenAI gpt-image-1.5/mini or Google Gemini 2.5 Flash Image (nano-banana) / 3 Pro Image. Bills per image returned (5-134 credits depending on model and quality). If you have your own provider API key configured, it is used (no internal credit charge); otherwise the platform key is used and credits are deducted.
chatConfig.autoAuthorizeInfo: When on, the agent installs and runs applications (and other sensitive actions) in this conversation without showing an authorization card. Applies to this conversation only.
chatConfig.advancedSectionDescription: Per-turn safety limits that prevent runaway agent loops.
chatConfig.maxPerResourcePerTurnInfo: Maximum number of creation calls the agent can make per resource type (agent, skill, sub-agent, interface, workflow, table) within a single turn. The same cap is applied independently to each type.
chatConfig.loopIdenticalStopInfo: Stop after this many identical consecutive model responses to prevent loops.
chatConfig.loopConsecutiveStopInfo: Stop after this many consecutive model steps to prevent runaway chains.
chatConfig.compactionEnabledInfo: Summarise older turns once the conversation grows long, to stay within the model's context window. Off by default; inherits the platform setting when left untouched.
chatConfig.compactionAfterTurnsInfo: Minimum number of new turns before the conversation is re-summarised. Lower means compaction runs more often.
chatConfig.compactionModelInfo: Which model writes the conversation summaries when compaction runs. Leave off to inherit: agent conversations use the agent's compaction model if set, otherwise the platform default (a cost-efficient model).

customApis.title: Custom APIs
customApis.subtitle: Register and manage your custom API integrations
customApis.emptyHint: Register a custom API to make it available for your agents and workflows.
customApis.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this API? This action cannot be undone.

aiHelpModal.title: Which API would you like to register?
aiHelpModal.description: Just tell us which API you need. The AI agent will register it end-to-end - base URL, auth, endpoints and output schemas - using sensible defaults from its knowledge of the provider.
aiHelpModal.placeholder: e.g. Stripe payments - to create and refund charges
aiHelpModal.promptIntro: Register a custom API in this user's catalog, end-to-end and as simply as possible. The user wants:
aiHelpModal.promptOutro: Call catalog(action='register_api', api_definition={...}) directly. Pick sensible defaults from your knowledge of the provider: official base URL, the standard auth type, 3 to 6 of the most useful endpoints, parameters with clear descriptions, and a complete outputSchema for each endpoint (every field typed and described). Do NOT ask the user to provide URLs, auth details, or endpoint paths - use what you already know. Only ask a clarifying question if a critical choice is genuinely ambiguous (e.g. which one of several equally plausible operations they meant). When done, reply with a one-line confirmation of what was registered.
